name: Flow-Like
what_it_does: A strongly-typed, local-first visual workflow automation platform built in Rust. Provides a drag-and-drop editor with 900+ built-in nodes for APIs, databases, file processing, and AI models. Runs on laptops, phones, servers, or edge devices with full offline capability and claims ~244K workflows/second throughput.
use_cases: Building enterprise automation workflows with a visual drag-and-drop editor
Running AI-integrated workflows locally without cloud dependencies
White-labeling a workflow platform with SSO, theming, and usage metering
Edge computing and offline-capable workflow execution
Data pipeline orchestration with type-safe transformations and audit trails
not_for: MCP server integration - no MCP support documented
Simple single-step automations that don't need a visual builder
Teams that prefer code-first over no-code workflow design
Organizations over 2000 employees or $300M ARR without enterprise license
best_when: You need a performant, local-first workflow engine with visual editing, type safety, and enterprise features like white-labeling and audit trails. The Rust-based engine delivers exceptional throughput.
avoid_when: You specifically need MCP server integration (none exists), prefer cloud-native workflow tools, or are a large enterprise that would require commercial licensing.

auth.notes: SSO/OIDC for user authentication, JWT for programmatic access. Per-tenant scoped secrets for multi-user deployments.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free for organizations under 2,000 employees and $300M ARR.
pricing.notes: Business Source License (BSL) - not truly open source. Free for qualifying smaller organizations, enterprise license required for large orgs.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: No MCP server exists. Documentation covers the workflow platform well but has no MCP-related content.
known_agent_gotchas: No MCP server support whatsoever - listed as MCP package but has none
BSL license restricts use for large organizations
Complex build requirements (Rust, Bun, Node.js, Python, Protobuf)
Primary value is the visual workflow editor, not AI agent integration

security_score_components.security_notes: Community/specialized tool. Apply standard security practices for category. Review documentation for specific security requirements.
